# Adamant Protocol

> A Layer 1 blockchain protocol — privacy by default, post-quantum cryptography, sub-second finality. No foundation, no premine, no admin keys, no on-chain governance. Adamant Move VM for narrow smart contracts with shielded execution.

Adamant is a base-layer settlement chain. It is the conjunction of seven properties that no existing programmable chain delivers in combination: privacy by default, post-quantum security, ≥50,000 TPS throughput floor, sub-second finality, encrypted mempool, phone-verifiability, and residential-fibre decentralisation.

The protocol is in the specification phase. There is no live chain. The whitepaper is the source of truth.

## Canonical sources

- [Whitepaper (markdown, 13 sections)](https://github.com/adamant-protocol/adamant-spec/tree/main/whitepaper)

- [/network/mempool](https://adamantprotocol.com/network/mempool) — two-regime encrypted mempool inspector
- [/network/burns](https://adamantprotocol.com/network/burns) — burn-to-mint registry
- [/network/genesis-nft](https://adamantprotocol.com/network/genesis-nft) — cohort marker + Genesis NFT registry
- [/network/charts](https://adamantprotocol.com/network/charts) — chain charts
- [/network/peers](https://adamantprotocol.com/network/peers) — peer map (ASN + geography)
- [/wallet](https://adamantprotocol.com/wallet) — reference wallet: balances, send/receive, view keys, history, address book, burn-to-mint, fees, recovery, hardware, mobile
- [/operator](https://adamantprotocol.com/operator) — operator dashboard for the four participation tiers
- [/status](https://adamantprotocol.com/status) — glanceable network health + incident log + public RPC list
- [/security](https://adamantprotocol.com/security) — threat model, cryptographic primitive provenance, audits, disclosure, bug bounty, reproducible builds
- [/updates](https://adamantprotocol.com/updates) — project journal pulled live from commit log
- [/community](https://adamantprotocol.com/community) — channels, ADIP improvement process, contributors
- [/faq](https://adamantprotocol.com/faq) — frequently asked questions
- [/glossary](https://adamantprotocol.com/glossary) — every term Adamant uses
- [/press](https://adamantprotocol.com/press) — press kit (logos, colours, talking points)
- [/legal](https://adamantprotocol.com/legal) — website terms + privacy policy
- [/license](https://adamantprotocol.com/license) — Apache 2.0

## Key parameters

- Genesis pool: 100,000,000 ADM, partitioned 70 / 30 between burn-to-mint and validator block rewards
- Burn-to-mint rate: 20 ADM per USD-equivalent (USDT/USDC 1:20; BTC/ETH calibrated at protocol design time, constant through launch — NOT a live oracle)
- Per-address claim cap: 1% m0–m1 → 2% m1–m3 → 4% m3–m6 → 8% m6–m12 → uncapped (WP §10.2.3)
- Launch phase cap: 5 years from genesis; unclaimed pool at cap is destroyed
- Active set: floor 7, ceiling 75 at launch (FCFS with mutual-consent transferable membership)
- Stake floors: 1,000 ADM Node Runner, 100 ADM Node Watcher (re-evaluated every 5 years per WP §11.5.4)
- Unbonding: 28 days (Node Runner), 7 days (Node Watcher)
- Throughput floor: ≥ 50,000 TPS at the launch active-set ceiling on residential-fibre commodity hardware
- Finality target: ~500 ms for owned-object transactions
- Block time target: ~8 seconds
- Token: ADM, native (not contract-defined), 10^-9 decimals; no fixed supply cap
- Security tiers: I (N=7–14), II (N=15–29), III (N≥30)
- Mempool: two-regime — threshold-encrypted at N≥15 validators (BLS on BLS12-381), time-lock VDF at N<15 (Wesolowski). Hysteresis: switch up at N≥15, down at N<10.
- Genesis cohort: first 75 active-set slots receive a permanent on-chain marker (non-transferable, stays with original address) + freely tradeable Genesis NFT (WP §8.1.9)
- Slot transferability: by mutual consent at an epoch boundary; the cohort marker does not transfer with the slot

## Cryptography

- Primary hash: SHA3-256, SHAKE-256 (FIPS 202) with BIP-340 tagged-hash domain separation
- Auxiliary hash: BLAKE3 (non-consensus-critical performance paths only)
- zk-friendly hash: Poseidon (in-circuit only)
- Classical signatures: Ed25519 (RFC 8032)
- Post-quantum signatures: ML-DSA-65 / FIPS 204 (CRYSTALS-Dilithium), 1952-byte pubkeys, 3309-byte sigs
- Aggregate signatures: BLS on BLS12-381 (validator vote aggregation)
- Symmetric: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (RFC 8439)
- Threshold encryption: BLS threshold on BLS12-381 (mempool, N≥15 regime)
- Time-lock encryption: Wesolowski VDF on RSA / class groups (mempool, N<15 regime)
- Post-quantum KEM: ML-KEM-768 / FIPS 203 (CRYSTALS-Kyber) — stealth addresses, encrypted memos
- Zero-knowledge proofs: Halo 2 (PLONKish, no trusted setup) — shielded execution + recursive verification
- Vector commitments: KZG on BLS12-381

## Adamant Move (VM)

Adamant ships a Move-based language and VM (Adamant Move + AVM). Objects are typed resources with declared mutability (IMMUTABLE | OWNER_UPGRADEABLE | VOTE_UPGRADEABLE | UPGRADEABLE_UNTIL_FROZEN | CUSTOM). Functions annotated #[shielded] compile to Halo 2 circuits; state transitions ship as zero-knowledge proofs. Mutability declarations are always cleartext, regardless of shielding (WP §6, §7.8).

## What Adamant is not

- Not a token launch — no premine, no foundation allocation, no investor round
- Not a general-purpose world computer — Move contracts pay gas; shielded functions carry proofs; the VM is narrow on purpose
- Not a governance system — no on-chain governance, no admin keys
- Not a scaling layer — base layer, not a rollup or sidechain
- Not a privacy add-on — privacy is structural, not opt-in
- Not novel cryptography — primitives are standard, peer-reviewed, audited

## License

Apache 2.0 (whitepaper, reference implementation, reference wallet, website).
